reviews.text:
I arrived at his office early for our appointment, as I am looking into filing for chapter 7 bankruptcy. He was unprepared for my visit; this was the first thing. Then we move into his office, where interruption after interruption was going happening, and he was moving away from our conversations repeatedly, to tend to these disruptions. I found this super annoying. You should arrange to order you medications in advance or have these conversations before or after your appointments with potential clients. This looked really bad. I am very unimpressed. Next, he was running me in circles with a civil matter I have currently, that I am trying to resolve, and changed back and forth from two different answers several times. And to top it all off, the vocal fry was very annoying to me as well.

I am a person with a disability, whom he asked what my disability is. And I was taken aback by that question, because you're not supposed to ask someone that. If they volunteer it, that's one thing. But you do not ask someone that question. I even mentioned that to him, as well.

I have contacted and met with another B.A. who will not pork me for $2,000.00 to file for chapter 7. This other B.A. does it on a sliding scale, according to someone's income. And he does this, because, well, you're filing for bankruptcy, how can you just pull $2,000.00 out of your pocket. There's a REASON why I am seeking services related to not having any money. This one will be much more affordable, so I can get what I need done.

This review will not be coming down, it will remain up. I would highly advise anyone seeking to file for chapter 7, look around. There are cheaper options available to you.

I gave 2 stars, because the office air conditioning was pleasant to me.

Sorry, but I am not going to be hiring you, Attorney David Osterman to represent me in a chapter 7 bankruptcy case.
An awesome lawyer, very knowledgeable about the bankruptcy process and an all around great person. I would highly recommend him to anyone I know.

David

I was in a very bad accident and I needed a lawyer,  and being new to NH, I looked online and picked the first legitimate one that I saw.  Attorney Kenison went through the process with me and my wife (we both had separate claims with him from the same accident).
You have to wait until all of your medical issues are dealt with,  psychologists, etc, then he put in the claim. He said he would try to get it done before the end of the first year,  but the only reason it didn't happen is because the adjuster on the other side didn't want to pay high money.  However I had a certain amount I wanted and when they finally came up with a number in was very low and I refused to accept until I got it,  I would have went to court if I had to.  So after the second year (you must have patience,  because it is not your attorneys fault,  but it could take up to three years for the claim to go through), attorney Kenison got for me the exact number that I wanted and an excellent price for my wife.  He is the real deal and will fight to get you what you want.  So if you are in the Manchester area,  give him a try,  he will not let you down.

The Kenison Law firm is the best of the best!!! John Kenison is a true professional and extremely knowledgeable. John answered all my questions and explained things so I could understand. He made me feel comfortable. If you need an attorney, I would highly recommend Attorney John Kenison.

Some of these were in fact outside his area of specialization, but nevertheless he was willing to spend time discussing them with me and ultimately recommending a lawyer who did specialize in them, and i was quite happy with the results. Sometimes legal intricacies and jargon can be quite intimidating to the layman, but John is very patient and thorough in explaining things; you always leave him with a better understanding of your legal situation and the knowledge that you not have been talked down to, but instead educated in the law as it applies to your case.  He genuinely cares for his clients and always seeks to get the best deal for them. I have recommended him to several of my friends and they have been universally pleased with the results. I really do not think you could find a better all-around car accident/personal injury lawyer than John.

reviews.text: Tremendous experience with personal injury/motor vehicle accidents.   Will always be able to talk to her directly and always returns phone calls, handling all aspects of your motor vehicle accident claim.  Honest and direct, highly recommended!!!
Deborah was scheduled to perform notary services for me.  She didn't show up.  No text, no call, no nothing.  Complete waste of time.  Totally unprofessional and disrespectful.
Attorney Cuomo is the best person for a will or trust.  She really knows how to plan an estate and looks out for your future. Don't hesitate to call her

Deborah Crompton Cuomo represented me in a horrific car accident with numerous medical bills, she was thorough and extremely responsive.  The accident was almost a year later upon reaching out to her.  She kept in touch via texts and was extremely responsive. She was knowledgeable in navigating through auto and health insurance companies and accepted very little from them. In the end , she surpassed our wishes and resolved all of our accounts. We are so grateful to her.

Debbie Crompton-Cuomo is one of the best personal injury attorneys in the Merrimack Valley area.  I have referred her to many of my family members and they all rave about her exceptional service and satisfaction with the end result. If you need a personal injury attorney, I highly recommend you contact Debbie.  5 stars all the way.

I have consulted Deb Coumo on several occasions about some very sensitive situations concerning a third party. She has always been timely and knowledgable with her response. I appreciate her advice and feel confident to recommend her to friends and family.
She is the real deal. Helped me and others that i have referred.

She was given my case June 2017 for personal injury.  I also referred my friend to he.

The level and frequency of contact to me was far below what I expected.   I did not know what she was doing.

So I sent a letter asking for information and documents.   I got a cryptic one page letter.

I wrote a second letter noting that her first letter was unresponsive.   I got a package back.  I didn't open it.

I texted today and found out she stopped representing me.

I referred her because of my mandatory referral requirements at BNI.

I find her treatment of me, especially as a lawyer, unprofessional and despicable as a whole.

No one should be treated like this.  Not even a goodbye telephone call.

I also sent her my son. She hardly had any contact with him. And she sent a letter of the demand without sending him a copy or interviewing him. She refused to provide him or me with a settlement agreement and the letter of demand until she was forced to do so. I asked her several times and once in a presence of my son and for complete copy of the file and that was in March and even before as far as I remember and today is September. I've gotten nothing. I sent her a final letter today to deliver my son's complete file. If I don't get it, I will file a complaint with the Board of Bar Overseers.

Such behavior is unacceptable professionally and personally. If I talk little of I think even less of her now.

Hire her at your own risk.

I was referred to Deborah in 2012 by a trusted friend for an injury sustained in a car accident. It was my first experience with an injury caused by another party, and I did not want a lawyer involved. However, at his encouragement, I made an appt to understand how she could help me. She was great from that first visit through receiving a settlement. She was professional, friendly, clear at explaining things and answering questions. I would highly recommend her!

I recommend her so much, that I had my daughter see her after a recent accident that totaled our car.

Deborah Cuomo represented me in a car accident personal injury case. Deborah was professional, prompt, and reliable -- she guided me through the entire proceeding in a way that made an intimidating process seem much more manageable and comfortable. She was very easy to work with and kept me updated on my case on a regular basis.

Ultimately, she came through for me in a major way and I felt she was a true advocate for me and my best interests. I would recommend her services to anyone and would not hesitate to use her again if needed.

Be aware this firm will waste your time. Me and my ex filed with Germaine & Blaszka, back in April of 2025 and by the end of 2025 nothing had moved forward. Between the constant calls and notes left with this firm and the emails that were sent and nothing returned or nothing progressing I have decided to look elsewhere. I would rather recommend anyone else then this firm.

Don Blaszka took my paperwork during the initial consultation so I couldn't go to any other attorneys for their pricing or opinions.  After, I felt almost forced into hiring him he proceeded to do the bare minimum for me because he was already paid.  Blaszka proceeded to be an hour and a half late to my court date and walked in like it wasn't a problem and forced a deal upon me at the last minute.

Lastly, I talked to his assistant regarding my case and asked for a call back. That callback never came.

Can confirm I will not recommend him to anyone for anything.

Do not give this guy your money.
Make up your own mind. Great if you have deep pockets.

Don managed to outperform his stellar reputation. Worth every penny. Very well connected in the state of NH and has earned the respect of those most important in law enforcement and the courts. Attorney Blazka is always outstanding when i have used him.He is thorough and honest, and his results have always been on the winning side for me.I have used him multiple times and sent family and friends his way. Him and his whole team never disapoint.🤘🙏
Just don’t waste your money (we had attorney DiTrapano)
Waste of money
Don is well versed in Law, quite energetic and has not disappointed me. His staff is just as friendly and helpful as he is.

They missed critical information that I had emailed them, then later said that information would have been helpful.

Then they just stopped responding one day. I was paid in full. The secretary would hear my name and hang up. I spent months trying to find out why, I emailed and called a few times, but they never gave me an answer. Will update this review if they respond.

I'm thrilled to share my positive experience with attorney Blaszka & Team.Their expertise and dedication were evident throughout my case, resulting in a successful outcome.I highly recommend their services for anyone in need of legal representation.
Ethan Thomas a client of theirs is an abhorrent person. Please avoid this firm.
Attorney Blaszka was easy to work with, and helped me tremendously in my case. Highly recommend.

Attorney John Korbey represented our non-profit organization during a real estate transaction in Southern NH. Attorney Korbey assisted me on a business acquisition, and I truly couldn’t have done it without him.  He did far more than just review and prepare legal documents – he provided excellent counsel, insight, suggestions, and advice as he has extensive experience in this area across many different industries.  He was always extremely responsive and has great communication skills, which was very important as he was able to distill many complicated scenarios into very simple terms so that I always understood everything I needed to provide decisions for.  Not only did he make the entire process smooth and efficient, but he also made it very enjoyable.  Every day I knew that Attorney Korbey had my best interests first, and having interacted with multiple attorneys during the course of this business transaction, I quickly realized that isn’t the case with all attorneys.  I highly recommend Attorney Korbey for anybody contemplating, or undergoing, a business acquisition and already look forward to working with him on my next one.

Don Blaszka is an exceptional attorney to have as counsel for your legal concerns. He is a staight forward no nonsense get it done representative. He concluded two cases in my favor to which two previous attorneys failed to provide. If you need strong legal assistance do not hesitate, call Don!

We cannot recommend Chrissy enough. From our very first consultation, it was clear that we were working with someone who combines exceptional expertise with genuine care for her clients. Her depth of knowledge in adoption law gave us complete confidence at every stage of the process. Chrissy anticipated challenges before they arose, explained our options clearly, and always had a well-thought-out strategy in place.
What truly set her apart was her amazing communication. She kept us informed every step of the way, responded promptly to every question (no matter how small), and explained complex legal details in a way that was easy to understand. We never felt left in the dark or unsure about what was happening.
Chrissy’s proactiveness made an enormous difference. She stayed ahead of deadlines, prepared us thoroughly for each milestone, and ensured that every document was accurate and complete. Because of her organization and forward-thinking approach, there were no last-minute surprises—only steady, confident progress.
Most importantly, she created a completely stress-free environment for our family. The adoption process can be overwhelming, but she carried the legal burden so gracefully that our only real job was to focus on being parents. We felt supported, protected, and truly cared for throughout the entire journey.
If you are looking for an adoption attorney who combines expertise, clear communication, and heartfelt dedication, Attorney Hanisco is simply the best. We are beyond grateful for her guidance and support and will being using her services in the future should we adopt again!

Thank you Chrissy from the bottom of our hearts! ❤️

My wife and I recently had the pleasure of working with Chrissy Hanisco for our Confirmatory Adoption needs. Since I was the one carrying, I wanted my wife, Elizabeth to have the same legal protections as  do regarding parentage in all 50 States.
Chrissy is incredibly knowledgeable on this subject and knows the laws inside-and-out. From our initial meeting, we knew she was the lawyer we wanted to represent our family. Before talking with Chrissy, we had spoken to one other Attorney and that Attorney could not be bothered reaching out to us personally—she had her assistant do so.

We got an incredibly personalized experience with Chrissy. Our initial consult was done through zoom (which was incredibly convenient)! Corresponding with her was a breeze, she kept us up-to-date through every step of the process. Even when there was a minor hiccup with the Nashua Court, Attorney Hanisco handled it professionally and filed a motion to proceed which the court accepted.
When we were first looking into this process, my wife and I were very nervous! Chrissy honestly made all the difference.  We recommend her to anyone going through adoption—whether in the LGBTQ+ Community or not.

We cannot thank her enough for assisting us in this process. We will be using her again for our future children as well as Estate Planning.

-Olivia and Elizabeth Bellemore

5 Stars isn't enough for George!  I honestly don't have enough words to express how thankful I am for him.  From our very first call when he took the time to just talk to me, not rushed, but genuinely cared and was concerned...like an old friend...just wanting to know what happened and how he could help me to the final outcome that exceeded my expectations...he was amazing!  He took the time to explain every step, answered all my questions with patience, and always made me feel like my case truly mattered.  His guidance, honesty, and dedication are unmatched.  I couldn't have asked for anyone better to represent me and would recommend George without hesitation to anyone who needs not just a great lawyer, but a truly caring one they can trust!  I'm incredibly grateful for everything George did for me!  No need to look further...You just found your guy!!!

We can't say enough good things about George as he represented our family in a medical malpractice matter. As a family we knew in our hearts that there was a case even when others said no. George carefully and meticulously reviewed the records and knew that justice needed to be served. Throughout the process George was very thorough and had great expert physicians available to consult with. He was articulate in explaining the process and law and was always available to answer our questions. George is a very kind and compassionate lawyer with strong morals and values and was an incredible guide through the entire case. We are very thankful that George was our legal representative and would highly recommend him to meet your legal needs.
George is a great guy! Very helpful and knowledgeable and he cares. He goes above and beyond to help people. The only attorney I would recommend!

George Spaneas was hired to represent my sister in a medical malpractice suit. Going into this we knew something was not right but we had no clue the extent of neglect until Mr. Spaneas took the case. He dove in like I don’t think any other lawyer would have. The amount of time he put into making sure my sister was not only compensated for such neglect but also made sure she was heard. To say he went above and beyond for my sister and our family is an understatement, Involving himself with anything that may have improved her quality of life. This may have been listening to her and letting her take her time instead of treating her slow, as others have done or Making sure she was seeing the right Drs. and getting the right tests. It would have been easier in most cases to just deal with me in discussing the case but no, he always kept her involved and would repeat anything she may not have understood the first time. I have never met anyone so passionate about doing the right thing.  Now that the case is behind us we are so proud to call him our friend. You will never find anyone better on your side fighting for you and your needs.

So glad I found Spaneas Law Office. Upon first meeting George it was evident how much he already cared about helping me through my malpractice suit and he handled my case professionally and personally. His genuine concern for my well being even after settling out of court will always be appreciated.
I definitely recommend using Spaneas law Office.

I am very thankful to have found George. He is willing to defend even the most complex cases. He is both confident, tenacious and strategic in his approach. He has won many big cases that some would argue were not "winable" if thats even a word :). He represented me extremely well and I couldnt have asked for a better lawyer. At the very least talk with him about your situation and I have no doubt that you to will find comfort in having him represent you.

I think it’s important that you have a connection with whoever you decide to hire. I can say without a doubt that I had an immediate connection with her. She is great with communication, and  even got back to me during non business hours on multiple occasions. She doesn’t tell you what you want to hear, or make promises she can’t keep. I would not of been able to handle the court hearings without her. We just wrapped up our custody battle, and I couldn’t be happier with the results.

Cathy, I seriously appreciate everything you’ve done, and all the battles you’ve fought for me and the girls. I wouldn’t of obtained this without you!

reviews.ownerResponse: To begin with, I want to apologize if you've received less than stellar customer service.  We pride ourselves on being easy to talk to, easy to reach, and hard working attorneys.  Beyond that, Personal Injury cases simply take time to settle properly.  If you're looking for a quick buck on a slip and fall- please look elsewhere, we can't help you with that.  But if you've been seriously injured from someone else's negligence, and you're receiving the medical care and treatment you need to recover form that- please DO get in touch to be fairly compensated for those unjust expenses and headaches! But honestly one has to complete their treatment in order to get the most money for their case.   This process therefore takes time and there often isn't much if anything to report for updates.  Ironically, it is the CLIENT who must keep their attorney updated most of the time!  Not only as to their treatment status, but also in response to the questions from the opposing side or insurance company.  While it can be a pain, it is a necessary part of the process.  Rest assured we are here to assist. And in any event, clients are always kept up date here and so the original attorney handling this particular matter was actually let go for communication issues.     Anyone who has worked with Keith or Jake at the firm will tell you they are exceptionally diligent and responsive. As noted above, these things take time. And finally, of course we would never ask someone to purger themselves...that is unequivocally denied.

I found Attorney St. Louis via a google search. He is an awesome Professional DUI attorney. We only chatted once via phone, so he could understand my DUI case. He stated an extra $150 credit card processing fee if paying his retainer fee vs. no extra fee if paying via a cashier/certified check to him. He's very responsive to email -or- text messages w/in 24 hours. I met him only 20 minutes in person prior to my arraignment date at  Rockingham court. He discussed with me his professional approach to working with the prosecutor on my DUI case. He was going to ask for negligence or reckless driving, as they are moving violations and to pay the fines which were better than DUI fines. I did not want to go to trial for DUI as I wanted closure today. I stressed numerous times that I can't afford to get my license revoked or suspended plus I'm unemployed and I do have a Government Secret clearance level. Furthermore, I did not want this as a red flag on my driving record as it has been clean as a whistle. We were in court for less than 10 minutes and the result was my DUI first offense case was dismissed by the prosecutor. Hooray!
Definitely, I would highly recommend Attorney Michael St. Louis and he's licensed in CT/MA/NH.

(which always before he was paid based off the winnings of the cases and he's a very good attorney if he knows he's going to make money) my experience with him being court appointed and working in a family custody case has been the worst experience. I put so much trust in John because I've seen him work hard. I had faith that he would get this situation resolved in a timely manner. unfortunately this experience has shown me that his efforts are not based off integrity and personal morals but financial gain. this is the most important case I've brought to him and he has not fought for me the way he needs to. there's been time he's gone against me as if he's working for the opposing side , and also BRIBED me in the beginning of the case to write good reviews online and he would work hard on my case which I have in text message. He is an excellent attorney and a great advocate. he is appointed by the court. He will do an excellent job for you."   now like I mentioned this has been the worst experience ever. I would not recommend him UNLESS you're paying him outta pocket and he knows he's going to make money. since he's been court appointed his work ethic is not the same. and its sad because like I said I've known John for YEARS !!! friends on Facebook and many many messages between him and I via Facebook messenger/text etc so this wasn't just a random lawyer. I had high expectations and put so much trust in him and had to face the consequences. if I was RICH enough I wouldn't still be fighting this case ten months later with no light at the end of the tunnel just a bunch of well this is how the system works and there's nothing I can do about it. I have delt with a lot of lawyers of all kinds over the years. Some will coddle you (while billing you); some will go out of their way to do mundane paperwork (while billing you); some will constantly call and talk to you about anything (while billing you). See the thread?

Randell isn't the type to run to up a bill with unnecessary things. He is honest and doesn't blow a lot of smoke. He gets to the point, does what he needs to do, and expects you to do the things you should be doing. When I get the documents in order that he asks for, everything turns out fine.

Just recently, I asked for his counsel on a new topic. He let me know that he could do it, but there was a better option for me. He even gave me the contact information for that option. I have never seen a lawyer pass off a client because it would be better for the client.

Anyway, long story short. If you are honest, he is too. Be prepared for the no BS answer, even when it is not what you want to hear. If you do your part, he will do his part, and he won't run up an unnecessary tab doing it.

I am a repeat customer, which is a first for me.

Howard was court appointed to me for my child custody. The hearing was scheduled four days after I had gotten my paperwork. Howard was appointed two days before the hearing. I gave him all the information I had gathered basically the day before my hearing. We spoke on the telephone briefly with me trying to fill him with information that he hadn't absorbed yet. He called me the following morning with a handful of questions and asked of any particulars that I deemed very important. With that, we spoke for a brief time prior to the telephonic hearing.

Once the alloted 30 minutes began, the experience poured out.. he addressed every issue that I felt important and truly killed it. Everything that was thrown our way, he cut apart and discredited the what the Plaintiffs tried putting together. Very impressed with his performance.

The court date had been on Friday and on Monday morning he emailed me and called me to inform me that the case was dismissed. For such short notice and how much he had to absorb, I couldn't ask for better representation.

No matter how busy his caseload he always treated us like we were his only client, he was quick to respond to emails and messages and always gave us his undivided attention and expertise.  A  seasoned legal expert, Jason is a gifted litigator and negotiator and I assure you he is the one you want in your corner when in need of legal assistance. reviews.ownerResponse: I am a mediator and trial lawyer whose work comes almost exclusively from regular known sources, primarily other lawyers and insurance companies.  I do not advertise or hold myself out as someone handling matters of the type described in this review, but I do occasionally gets a cold call from someone like Mr. Kang who has a small consumer related problem for which hiring me is not economical because they would spend more money on fees than they are fighting over.  I usually give them a few minutes of my time and try to direct them to free or reduced-fee sources for legal services, or to someone who handles the type of problem they have.  I have on very rare occasions done a “lawyer’s letter” at low cost for someone in a jam to see if it prompts a favorable response for them.  While I have no memory of Mr. Kang, I am 100% certain that I have never told someone I would help them and then reversed course and treated them in the way he describes in his review.  I am sorry to hear how he was treated.  Some calls of this type come from people who are doing broad internet searches and perhaps Mr. Kang has confused me with someone else he talked with during the time he was dealing with the problem he was wrestling with.  It definitely was not me.

reviews.text:
I cannot say enough good things about McGrath Law Firm, and especially Peter. From our very first consultation, Peter made it clear that our best interests were his top priority. He was not only an excellent attorney but also genuinely kind and compassionate. You could tell he truly cared about us as people, not just as clients.

Peter and the entire team demonstrated exceptional professionalism throughout our time working together. They took the time to explain every step of the process in terms we could understand and were always available to answer our questions - no matter how small. Their communication was outstanding; we never felt left in the dark or unsure about what was happening.

What impressed us most was Peter's commitment to protecting us. He was thorough, strategic, and always thinking several steps ahead. Peter was also strong and assertive when it mattered most - he didn't let anyone push us around and stood firm in advocating for our interests. We felt confident knowing he had our back every step of the way.

Their pricing was fair and transparent - no surprise fees or hidden costs. They were upfront about everything from the beginning, which we greatly appreciated.

If you're looking for a law firm that truly cares about their clients and will fight for you with integrity and skill, I wholeheartedly recommend McGrath Law Firm and Peter. They earned our trust and our gratitude.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your thoughtful review, K! We’re truly grateful for your trust and kind words. Our team — and Peter in particular — is dedicated to providing compassionate, strategic, and results-driven legal representation. Whether it’s family law, probate, estate matters, business law, or civil litigation, we take pride in guiding our clients with integrity, clear communication, and personal attention every step of the way. We’re honored to have been able to help and appreciate you sharing your experience.

Thank you, Kristi, for the 5-star review! We truly appreciate your support. Our team takes pride in providing trusted, results-driven representation in family law, probate and estate matters, business law, real estate, and civil litigation across South Carolina and New Hampshire. We’re grateful you chose McGrath Law Firm and look forward to assisting you whenever you need legal guidance in the future.

reviews.text:
In a previous review I defined my experience a Dan McGrath law office as "old world".  Dan McGrath is a knowledgeable, pleasant, and likable person.  And a visit to his office is a wonderful experience.  The "old world" experience, is Dan still used the dictaphone technology.  The Dictaphone recorder was cutting technology back when Joe Dolan played the Ranch House in Cummer in the early 1970s, twenty five years before the internet.  It requires recording, document creation, reviews and edit, correction reviews ....
During my father's land transfer an error in a document required all parties to return to the office a week later to sign the finalized documents.  Such old world experience was a total was of five person's time, and my father died only days later.
Today's technogy  for document creation and appointment scheduling makes such old world dictaphone obsolete.  Also, the old world office has the a wall lined with redundant law books to indicate some acquired knowledge. Such information is easily found online.

Peter and his team helped me during one of my most difficult times in my life. I was facing issues from two entirely separate areas, divorce, and my credit. He made sure I wasn't taken advantage of, and resolved both issues timely and efficiently. Peter and his team are a great firm who can help in virtually any area. I'd highly recommend them.

I highly recommend Tom Doyle for anyone navigating the complex and often overwhelming process of a disability case. From our very first consultation to the successful resolution of my claim, Tom demonstrated an unmatched blend of professional expertise and genuine compassion.

Navigating disability law felt impossible before meeting Tom. He possesses an intricate and up-to-date knowledge of both the SSA regulations and the necessary medical documentation required for a strong case. Tom took the time to explain every step, translating dense legal jargon into clear, actionable advice. His strategy was meticulous; he ensured all deadlines were met and that my application was as robust and complete as possible, ultimately saving me time and reducing the stress involved in the appeals process.

Beyond his legal acumen, Tom’s communication skills were outstanding. He was consistently responsive to my questions and concerns, always returning calls promptly and making me feel heard and valued, rather than just another case file. His empathetic approach during the most difficult parts of my journey provided critical reassurance. It was clear that he genuinely cared about my well-being and the outcome of my case.

Thanks to Tom Doyle’s dedication, skill, and strategic approach, my disability claim was approved. I firmly believe that without his guidance, the outcome would have been far less favorable. If you are seeking a determined, knowledgeable, and compassionate attorney to handle your disability case or other legal issues, look no further than Tom Doyle. He is a truly outstanding advocate.

Thomas, Tommy as I have got to know him. Has been an amazing lawyer through and through, he got me the maximum settlement in my case. I didn’t even need to show my face in a court room not once. He went above and beyond, I barely needed to lift a finger. I could not recommend him more!!!

When I was in a car accident a couple years back , I got referred to Tom and I’m so glad I did. Tom is very knowledgeable and understanding , he explains in detail next steps. I can’t thank him enough for pushing through and giving me the best outcome. Thanks Tom

Usually when you have to reach out to an attorney, it’s because your world turned upside down because something awful occurred. When fate unfortunately hit me with an awful, pain inducing legal issue, I reached out to Tom. Immediately after sharing what occurred, it was evident that he cared tremendously and led with a strong moral compass. He isn’t just about the paycheck for himself, and he’s not afraid to fight the good fight if it’s in his clients’ best interest. Due to the nature of my case, I had to share very personal details about my life that most would want to keep private. However, working with Tom made it easy, because he is compassionate, like a caring friend would be- not an intimidating/stiff attorney. When working with Tom, I often felt like I was his only client, even though I wasn’t! My matter started in 2020, however Tom still calls me every few months just to see how I’m doing. Most people don’t realize how nasty lawsuits are- the other side putting you down any way possible to boost their client’s narrative. It’s far from fun, but having a guy like Tom in your corner makes it a lot better!

Mr Doyle is the best attorney in his field as far as I am concerned. I'll never forget the day I met him he went out of his way to help me when he didn't have to do anything at all instead he listened and then instructed me on what to do and would proceed if I followed through with what he had told me to do.That was over ten years ago and he's still always kept in touch to make sure I was doing alright and I always appreciated that!Well not only is he a great and very competent lawyer he's a caring and compassionate human being!
Mr Doyle is the greatest chance meeting in my life
ever and can't thank him enough for all his help!!

Although I have been separated from my husband for two years and no longer had any legal rights to the property we once shared an issue arose which required my signature for a bank. I was confused about the need for my signature and the potential pitfalls for me in the future.  I had a tight timeline to determine my options and reached out to a few law offices for consultation.  Len Foy was the only one who replied to me and he did so immediately.  He was able to review the papers and give me legal advice with a couple of different options. His feedback made me feel secure with respect to dealing with the issue.

I highly recommend Len Foy to you as a lawyer who is very experienced and knowledgeable regarding legal issues and real estate transactions.  His office also handles estate planning, wills, and trusts.  His manner is friendly, supportive and professional.  Communication is clear and you won’t need to translate any legal jargon.  Beyond that he exudes a sense of warmth and integrity that is a breath of fresh air.   If the opportunity arose I would happily consult with Len again.

All I can say is wow! I worked closely with Julia Foy and she was nothing short of amazing! I can’t count how many times I thanked her throughout the process of selling my house because there were just too many times I was so grateful for her. She made me feel empowered and like someone always had my back. An absolute professional in every way and so efficient. I’m beyond impressed and I can’t sing her praises enough. Foy law did a great job handling a quick close. Highly recommend!

Julia went above and beyond—ensuring every detail was handled correctly, thoroughly explaining each step, and even helping my family member connect with the right professionals to support them on their end of the transaction.

I truly appreciate the time and effort she put in to make sure everything was done right. I highly recommend Julia to anyone in need of a real estate attorney, especially in situations where extra support and clarity are needed.

My first experience with Foy Law office started in January 2025, during the purchase of my mobile home in Greenville, NH. What a smooth process from beginning to end. The closing was so delightful and well organized.
The second part of my process was the selling of my home, I was downsizing. Both Len and Julia were so thorough with all the details, paperwork and the processing. They both expressed very friendly, positive feedback for every step I needed to complete the closing process. They always were available at any time I reached out to them for questions. I knew that I was in good hands making this a very pleasant and timely process. I highly recommend Foy Law office as real estate attorney, they receive a A+ rating from me. Thank you for everything!
Very easy to work with. Detailed oriented which I appreciate when needing an attorney.
I would highly recommend Len and his team for real estate transactions. Their service, professionalism, and communication was impeccable throughout our recent home purchase.

Very satisfied with this law office and its assistance with our home purchase in the Upper Valley region (Lebanon/Hanover, NH)! The team truly prioritizes the client’s needs while being highly knowledgeable, efficient, and providing the legal expertise one seeks.

This is the perfect-sized office with a strong focus on real estate—well-organized and responsive. The financial transactions were handled smoothly, reliably, and on time, with very reasonable pricing (much lower than other offices that charge exorbitant fees for checking easements or preparing ownership agreements).

It was well worth reaching out across the entire state of New Hampshire—our lawyer even traveled to our town for the closing meeting, so distance was not an issue. We were able to avoid unnecessary complications and secure legal representation we were fully satisfied with!
The firm provided great communication and advocacy. The process was smooth and the advice sound.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 11/10 - Exceptional Legal Representation

After getting injured on the job, I found myself in an incredibly difficult situation. My supervisor was hesitant to accept responsibility, especially since another company had equipment on site. While I was still in the ER, I was told, “Every injury is preventable, therefore unacceptable. This didn’t happen on our equipment—so we’re not paying for it.” I knew immediately I needed legal support.

That’s when I contacted Dennis at Shillen Mackall Seldon & Spicer—and I’m so grateful I did. Dennis got to work immediately and even traveled to Pennsylvania to help build my case. When my employer refused to cover my medical bills and denied my workers’ comp benefits, Dennis fought tirelessly to hold them accountable.

The entire team—Dennis, Kim, and Jack—was outstanding. They communicated clearly, kept me informed every step of the way, and made sure the process was as smooth as possible. They also caught my employer underreporting my hourly wage by nearly $6, which would have significantly reduced what I was owed. Thanks to their dedication and diligence, I received the compensation I deserved.

If it weren’t for Shillen Mackall Seldon & Spicer, I’d be stuck paying medical bills out-of-pocket and working a job that didn’t value my well-being. Instead, I had a team that genuinely cared and fought for my rights.

Highly recommend them for any personal injury case. Professional, compassionate, and relentless in their pursuit of justice.
11/10 stars.

My step-son got into some trouble due to driving without a license.  I called Nicholas Howie to try and see what he could do to help avoid a one-year loss of license ordered by the state.    Mr. Howie really took the time necessary to understand our case and my step-son’s situation as a recovering addict.   Mr. Howie’s price to help resolve the case was also very reasonable despite the complicated issues.   At one point, due to these issues, the state was unwilling to budge on the loss of license.  This was a horrifying situation as my step-son was finally making progress against his addiction and losing his license would not only cause him to lose his job but he would be unable to make his clinic appointments that are necessary to fight his addiction.  This was a life threatening issue and the state could care less.  This is when Mr. Howie got creative.  Despite the harsh push back from the prosecutor and without changing his price Mr. Howie continued to negotiate over several weeks and finally, with my step-son’s approval of the terms, came to a solution that was accepted by the state.  Negotiating terms aren’t always pretty but the end result was my step-son kept his license, kept his job, and continues to make his clinic appointments every day.  I’m convinced that due to Mr. Howie’s experience and relentless effort not to give up, when I know at several times he could have, that my step-son now has a second chance that has likely saved his life, thanks to Mr. Howie.

Joel never gave up on me or my case, and I am beyond grateful for his dedication and expertise. The work that I do  does involve emotional and stressful circumstances which can cause clients to feel like they are not being heard by the system and the people they hire to help them. reviews.text: Kristen is a very skilled and empathetic lawyer. She takes time to listen to her clients and she works with you to create realistic goals for your case. With all that said, when she's with you in the courtroom, that's when she really shines: she's sharp, precise, and she fights hard for you. Time and time again, when court staff heard her name as my lawyer, they would say, "you're in good hands." Indeed, Kristen is unique. She is very personable and insightful; she actually cares about you and your situation. I never felt like she was rushing me or pressuring me. She always explained the legal jargon very clearly and concisely, even some of the more complicated aspects of law became more transparent with her explanations. I felt valued as a client and in general. I recommend Kristen as a lawyer, especially if your concern relates to family law. At least in my experience, she articulated the particulars of the case and the matters of law as it pertained to it very clearly. I knew what to expect every step of the way and I never felt left out. She always factored in my opinion and desires for what I hoped for regarding the case. And if it was an unrealistic expectation, Kristen told me so, which I appreciated because I knew she was being honest and upfront. Kristen and her staff always made me feel welcome at the office. Each meeting was always informative. If I had a question, they took time to answer it fully. And if need be, they would repeat particular points. As you can imagine, family law is by far the most stressful area of law for anyone dealing within this realm. But regardless of this, Kristen always maintained composure and professionalism. Thank you to Kristen and the rest of the staff for assisting with my case.

I wish I could say the experience was pleasant but it was unfortunately incredibly arduous (however it wasn't from the beginning). I had used Attorney Weber for a previous case and she had done an astounding job building it for me and getting it dismissed almost as fast as it was falsely brought up. With this experience in hand, I immediately carried her over to a divorce and the quality dropped immensely as time went on.

I had to submit exhibits to their team multiple times, to which they had no centralized place to house them (which lead to multiple requests for the same exhibits). It became so tedious that I created a Google drive for their entire team to be able to access it. On multiple occasions (since I was out of state), they would mail me items that needed to be hand signed as a priority, but sent them to me via snail mail, often last minute and down to the wire.

There was one scenario where they mailed it via usps with no tracking during Hurricane Milton (which I had warned I was in the path of), where it was promptly lost. I had to then print out everything, sign it, and expedite overnight everything *back* to them, costing me hundreds of dollars because the court required the documents the next day.

The court days alone felt like they were prepped minutes before going to the judge, oftentimes right before walking into the court. Even when I was waiting for the judge to make a final verdict afterwards, I could not get their team to answer me without them taking at least a week to respond. I ended up calling the courts myself and finding the verdict on my own. I hate being the person to leave bad reviews, but when you pay 5 figures to have a lawyer represent you, you expect preparedness and responsiveness at the LEAST.

I did not receive that.
reviews.ownerResponse: Kyle, thank you for your comments.  I  noted that the overall outcome of your cases was positive.  We will continue to strive to make improvements for delivering our services under what can be difficult circumstances.

In 2021, my vehicle was struck on the passenger side by someone driving a stolen pickup truck, almost flipping over my SUV. My daughter was in the backseat, and the pickup truck hit her passenger door. Resulting in my daughter having some broken bones, and I was left with an injured back. My SUV was totaled. The individual driving the stolen vehicle did not have insurance, so I had to file a claim with my insurance company, and they were not very helpful.
I did a Google search for the best respected and reviewed lawyer, and I came across Charles Teale at Teale Law. From our first meeting, he was respectful, well-mannered, and professional. I felt comfortable speaking with Chris (Charles) Teale, and he was not intimidating like other lawyers can be. He dressed like an average person, casual and not in a business suit. That made it easy to explain what my daughter and I had just endured. He explained the process in detail and ensured I understood everything I signed. Chris took the stress off my shoulders by dealing with the insurance company. I was constantly informed of what was going on with each case. He negotiated a fair settlement for my daughter and I, more than I thought we would have received. Teale Law was prompt in responding to any questions I had. Chris more than exceeded my expectations, and I highly recommend Teale Law. I will use him in the future, and he was a pleasure to work with.

“Exceptional Attorney – Trusted Across All Areas of Law!”

From complex real estate transactions tied to divorce cases, to civil suits, and even criminal law matters, Keith has consistently gone above and beyond.

What truly sets Keith apart is his personal touch. He takes the time to understand not just the case, but the people involved. He’s approachable, patient, and genuinely cares about delivering the best outcome. His calm, confident demeanor instantly puts you at ease, and his ability to explain even the most complicated legal situations in a clear, straightforward way is invaluable.

As a real estate agent, I’ve referred clients to Keith knowing they’ll be in excellent hands—and he’s never disappointed. He’s been a trusted advisor for me and countless others, and I would recommend him to anyone needing help with real estate law, civil disputes, family matters, or criminal defense.

I had an outstanding experience with Attorney Dennis Hogan on my case.  His prior experience as a prosecutor was instrumental in helping me with my case.  I was charged with a DUI, but refused the test and was also charged with two other counts for Simple Assault and Reckless Conduct.  My court date was scheduled, but we were trying to change bail conditions and that date was 2 weeks prior to the 'real' court date.  At this earlier court appearance, Attorney Hogan was able to negotiate a plea deal where I plead guilty to the DUI and got the other 2 charges dropped, pending a 12 month probation period.  This allowed me to just close the book on this nightmare that I created for myself, accept the penalty for the DUI which I would have been found guilty of anyway, and make the other charges go away.  While I have not gotten my final bill from Attorney Hogan, this expedited process should also save me on my legal fees with him.  I would HIGHLY RECOMMEND Attorney Hogan to anyone else looking for a great attorney that is fair on costs and has the previous experience and knowledge as an elected prosecutor. He knows most of the current prosecutors which gives him (and you) a very real advantage over other attorneys.  Thank you Attorney Hogan!

If they are a real company who are trying their hardest and the victim of scammers using their names to scam people. Than oh well sucks for them. After 3 broken financial contracts with a partner I believed to be trust worthy I realized I was in over my head and needed legal counsel. I found John Deachman and his firm. John and his colleagues worked as a team to remedy the situation in a professional, intelligent, honorable and timely manner. I finally felt that not only did someone have my back but also a sincere interest in the best possible outcome for me and my family. I will be forever grateful to the team at Craig, Deachman & Associates, especially John Deachman, Shawn Hrbek and Mark van Zanten!
Attorney John Deachman practices with honesty, trustworthiness, and integrity.  This firm is top notch!  Very professional and responsive to clients needs.  I recommend this firm.

I have known W. John Deachman for years.  He has helped me through countless legal matters.  He handled each one with care and concern and always made me feel that he had my back.  He’s knowledgeable and has the ability to break things down into layman’s terms.  I would highly recommended him to anyone looking for fair, down to earth, honest legal counsel.

My family had to submit a petition to partition property in NH when an opportunistic co-owner tried to roadblock the sale of the home.  Attorney John Deachman did a fantastic job drafting & submitting the lawsuit to probate court.  He also managed all communication with both parties clearly & effectively.  We ended up not having to go to court & was able to strong-arm the co-owner into signing his interest over to my family because he did not want to deal with the possible consequences Atty Deachman laid out in the petition.  As an end result, we ended up with 100% ownership of this particular property & are now able to manage this property without any interference.  We highly recommend John Deachman’s expertise.

I was looking for information on a legal matter...5 seconds after my inquiry at 9:30 in the evening I received a call from attorney Amann.
After a brief conversation in which I provided him with all the facts of the case and agreed to send him the case file. After two weeks I called him to arrange a consultation...he still hadn't even looked at my file. Another week goes by and I have to leave him a voicemail about a consultation...he returned a very rude reply stating he couldn't work for free and before he would schedule an appointment I would have to see his paralegal and pay a fee for a consultation...never has any attorney required me for a fee before...a consultation is for me to interview him to make sure he is the right man for the case. So to end let me state that William Amann is the poster boy for so many lawyer jokes. Rude unprofessional and arrogant...I wouldn't hire him to shine my shoes.
reviews.ownerResponse: Attorney Amann has not been associated with the firm for a number of years.

The new judges dismiss your case without the tenant's even complaining about the eviction, if we make one error on the paper work - dismissed, not a peoples court anymore - I just recommend Attorney Marc to a life long friend

I’m very grateful for the care and support he showed throughout the case. He truly works in the best interests of his clients.

He waited until I spoke with him on the phone the second time where he was pressuring me to take my case to jury and pay him more money. After I told him I just wanted the plee deal I payed him for he handed me off to his paralegal whom he didn't even meet with because she didn't know it was my 2nd offense when she showed up in court.
Very disappointed. I made decisions based off the legal advice I received from Bowser Law that are still negatively impacting my quality of life.

Anytime the judge asked him something he was able to answer immediately and professionally.  I received the best possible outcome I could have expected.

Mike Bowser saved my future and changed the course of my life. Watching him work was one of the most impressive things I’ve ever seen.

Mike is the best, look no further. 19 months ago I swerved to avoid a deer at night. I missed the deer but I hit a rock on the right side of my vehicle which did damage to the right front suspension. That night would change my life forever because I was charged with OUI, having a loaded firearm in my possession, which I was licensed to carry and two other charges. I have never been is this situation, but I knew I needed an attorney and a good one so the search was on. After calling several of the best rated OUI attorneys I was discouraged until I spoke to Mike. When I first met with Mike, it was a free one hour consultation where he explained the whole process to litigate these charges and defend me. At this point, he had limited information so there was no sugar coating the severity of this case (the good, bad and ugly). Mike is an optimist and a realist at the same time, he puts you at ease and explains everything right up front. I felt he was the most honest attorney with a winning and positive attitude that would give me the best defense because he truly is an expert on OUI cases. There was a number of setbacks beyond his control which is inherent in the court system, but he kept me informed and we met when it was necessary. Throughout the process he tells you this is your case, and you are ultimately in charge of what course-of-action we take, based on his guidance and experience. I was found “Not Guilty” by a jury trial. In the end, all I can say is this, if you need an OUI attorney in Massachusetts call Mike Bowser, because he is the best and priced the fairest by far.
Would highly recommend to a friend.
